channel.title: Ebola Hub — 2026 DRC Bundibugyo Outbreak Tracker
channel.description: Live tracker for the 2026 DRC Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak. WHO updates, regional risk, vaccine pipeline, US airport screening, full timeline.

item.title: Day 8 of PHEIC: DRC Confirmed Climbs to 101 (106 Total with 5 Uganda Imports), 904 Suspected, 220 Deaths Cumulative per WHO DG; Africa CDC Names 10 At-Risk Countries; MSF Isolation Units Operational in Ituri
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: Monday May 25, 2026 is Day 8 after the WHO PHEIC declaration. DRC 101 PCR-confirmed (up from 83), Uganda 5 PCR-confirmed in Kampala (up from 2) for 106 cumulative confirmed; 904 suspected cases; 220 cumulative deaths according to the WHO Director-General. Africa CDC names 10 at-risk countries: Angola, Burundi, CAR, Republic of Congo, Ethiopia, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, Zambia. MSF isolation units operational in Ituri.

item.title: Africa CDC Names 10 At-Risk Countries: What the New Regional Watch List Actually Means for Border Surveillance, Travel and the Next 30 Days
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention has formally named ten African countries at heightened risk of importation from the 2026 DRC + Uganda Bundibugyo Ebola cluster. The named-country list defines the operational watch envelope for the next 30 days of the regional response.

item.title: Bundibugyo vs Zaire Ebolavirus: Why the 2026 DRC Strain Behaves Differently and What That Means for the Vaccine, Antibody and Antiviral Pipeline
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: Bundibugyo virus and Zaire ebolavirus differ at the surface glycoprotein, and those structural differences carry direct operational consequences. Ervebo, Inmazeb and Ebanga are not expected to work against the 2026 Bundibugyo strain; the WHO R&D Blueprint has prioritised two Bundibugyo-specific or pan-orthoebolavirus mAbs for clinical trial.

item.title: Uganda Cluster Grows to 5 Confirmed Cases in Kampala: What the Three Weekend Confirmations Mean for the Cross-Border Response and the Next Two Weeks
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: Three additional PCR-confirmed Bundibugyo cases in Kampala brought the Uganda cluster to 5 confirmed cases with 1 death. All five are travel-associated to DRC. No community-acquired secondary cases identified yet. The next two weeks cover the operationally decisive serial-interval window.

item.title: Ebola Symptoms by Day: The Bundibugyo Prodromal-to-Haemorrhagic Phase Timeline and the One-Sentence Rule That Defines Same-Day Care
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: The day-by-day Bundibugyo Ebola clinical timeline from Day 0 exposure through prodromal, gastrointestinal, and haemorrhagic phases — and the one-sentence rule for same-day medical evaluation in or after travel to affected regions.

item.title: WHO IHR Emergency Committee Temporary Recommendations: What the 22 May Document Actually Asks Countries to Do at Each Risk Level
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: The WHO IHR Emergency Committee, meeting for the first time on 22 May, issued temporary recommendations under the International Health Regulations (2005) covering the 2026 DRC + Uganda Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak. Graded by country risk level. Concrete operational asks at each tier.

item.title: Day 6 of PHEIC: WHO Upgrades DRC Area Risk to Very High, IHR Emergency Committee Issues Temporary Recommendations, Confirmed Cases Climb to 87 (~750 Suspected, 177 Deaths Cumulative)
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: Saturday May 23, 2026 is Day 6 after the WHO PHEIC declaration. At the 22 May joint Ebola + hantavirus media briefing the WHO upgraded the DRC area risk from "high" to "very high" (global remains low) and the WHO IHR Emergency Committee, meeting for the first time the same day, issued temporary recommendations. DRC confirmed cases now stand at 85 (+2 Uganda imports), suspected ~750, with 177 deaths cumulative. WHO R&D Blueprint prioritised two monoclonal antibodies for trial and obeldesivir for post-exposure prophylaxis evaluation.

item.title: Is There Ebola in the United States in 2026? What CDC + DHS Enhanced Airport Screening Actually Does
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: As of 23 May 2026 there are no community-acquired Ebola cases in the United States. CDC and DHS have implemented enhanced entry screening at 5 international airports — ATL, JFK, EWR, IAD, ORD — for arrivals from DRC and Uganda since 18 May. The American physician Dr Peter Stafford was medically evacuated to a German high-containment unit.

item.title: Ebola Symptoms in 2026: The Bundibugyo Prodromal, GI and Hemorrhagic Phases — and When to Seek Same-Day Care
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: Bundibugyo virus disease runs through three overlapping clinical phases. Incubation 2 to 21 days; sudden-onset fever, headache and body pain; GI vomiting and diarrhea by days 4–10; hemorrhagic features in 30–50% of severe cases by days 7–14. The operational rule: same-day evaluation for any fever lasting more than 24 hours with epidemiological exposure.

item.title: Is There an Ebola Vaccine in 2026? Why Ervebo Is Zaire-Specific and What the WHO Bundibugyo Pipeline Actually Looks Like
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: The Merck Ervebo vaccine is licensed for Zaire ebolavirus, not the Bundibugyo strain at the centre of the 2026 DRC outbreak. The Inmazeb and Ebanga monoclonal antibodies are similarly Zaire-specific. On 22 May 2026 the WHO R&D Blueprint formally prioritised two Bundibugyo-targeted monoclonal antibody candidates for clinical trial and the antiviral obeldesivir for post-exposure prophylaxis evaluation.

item.title: Obeldesivir and Two Monoclonal Antibodies: What the WHO R&D Blueprint Pipeline Actually Looks Like for Bundibugyo
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: At the 22 May media briefing, the WHO Director-General confirmed two operational R&D priorities for the DRC Bundibugyo cluster: two monoclonal antibody candidates prioritised for clinical trial, and the antiviral obeldesivir under evaluation in a clinical trial as post-exposure prophylaxis for high-risk contacts. Here is what each of those pipelines actually looks like.

item.title: Bundibugyo Virus in Goma: Why a Single Confirmed Case in a Regional Transit Hub Matters More Than the Count Suggests
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: The most operationally consequential geographic development in the 2026 DRC Bundibugyo outbreak is the documented spread into Goma, the North Kivu capital on the Rwandan border. Even a single sustained transmission chain there would meaningfully elevate cross-border risk.

item.title: Day 5 of PHEIC: DRC Bundibugyo Cluster Reaches 51 PCR-Confirmed Across 11 Health Zones, Spread Into North Kivu (Butembo, Goma) and South Kivu Import
item.dc:creator: Ebola Hub
item.description: Friday May 22, 2026 is Day 5 after the WHO PHEIC declaration on the DRC Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak. The cluster total stands at 51 PCR-confirmed cases in DRC plus 2 imported cases in Kampala (one death), approximately 600 suspected cases and 139 deaths reported cumulatively across 11 health zones in Ituri Province and North Kivu Province plus the single South Kivu import from Tshopo Province reported on May 21.
